prerequisites: A running PostgreSQL server reachable from the connector, with its hostname and port (default 5432) known.
The name of the specific database to read from.
A PostgreSQL user (role) with login privileges and at least SELECT on the tables and schemas you want to sync.
Network access from the connector to the database host, either directly or through an SSH bastion (set via tunnel_method).
A replication method chosen up front: standard (cursor-based) reads, or logical replication / CDC. CDC requires the user to be able to read from the database's logical replication stream.
For SSL client-certificate verification (ssl_mode), the CA certificate plus a client certificate and client key.
For SSH tunneling, reachable bastion host details and either an SSH private key or the tunnel user password.
For Microsoft Entra service-principal authentication on Azure Database for PostgreSQL, the Entra tenant ID and client ID.

authMethods.name: Username and password
authMethods.summary: Authenticate with a PostgreSQL role name (username) and its password. The server side enforces the password method (for example scram-sha-256 or md5) through pg_hba.conf. This maps to the connector's username config field and password secret field.

authMethods.name: SSL/TLS with client certificate
authMethods.summary: Encrypt the connection and optionally authenticate the client using certificates. The ssl_mode object supplies a CA certificate, client certificate, client key, and an optional client key password. libpq sslmode values control whether the server certificate is verified.

authMethods.name: SSH tunnel
authMethods.summary: Reach a database that is not directly exposed by tunneling through a bastion host. The tunnel_method object authenticates to the SSH host with either an SSH private key (ssh_key) or the tunnel user's password (tunnel_user_password).

authMethods.name: Microsoft Entra service principal
authMethods.summary: On Azure Database for PostgreSQL, authenticate using a Microsoft Entra (Azure AD) service principal instead of a database password. Set entra_service_principal_auth and provide entra_tenant_id and entra_client_id.

setupSteps.title: Create a database user for the connector
setupSteps.body: On the PostgreSQL server, create a dedicated login role and grant it the access it needs, for example CREATE USER with a password, then GRANT SELECT on the target tables and GRANT USAGE on their schemas. Use this role's name and password in the username and password fields.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connection target
setupSteps.body: Set host to the database server hostname or IP, port to the listening port (5432 by default), and database to the name of the database you want to read. Optionally restrict reads to specific schemas, and pass extra JDBC options through jdbc_url_params if needed.

setupSteps.title: Choose a replication method
setupSteps.body: Set the required replication_method field. Standard replication reads tables with cursors. Log-based CDC reads changes from the database's logical replication stream; the configured user must be able to read from it.

setupSteps.title: Decide on a network path
setupSteps.body: The tunnel_method field is required. Choose a direct connection when the connector can reach the database host, or an SSH tunnel through a bastion. For an SSH tunnel, provide the SSH host details plus either ssh_key (private key) or tunnel_user_password.

setupSteps.title: Enable SSL/TLS if required
setupSteps.body: Configure ssl_mode to control encryption and certificate verification. For verifying the server, supply ca_certificate; for mutual TLS, also supply client_certificate, client_key, and, if the key is encrypted, client_key_password. SSL behavior follows the standard libpq sslmode semantics.

setupSteps.title: Configure Entra authentication for Azure (optional)
setupSteps.body: If connecting to Azure Database for PostgreSQL with a Microsoft Entra service principal, enable entra_service_principal_auth and set entra_tenant_id and entra_client_id. Otherwise leave these fields empty and rely on username and password.

sources.title: PostgreSQL Documentation: Database Connection Control Functions (libpq)
